raw_result.description: Plaid provides APIs that let developers connect apps to users’ financial accounts and access banking data and payment capabilities.
raw_result.auth_notes: Plaid documents request authentication using a client_id and secret sent in headers or the request body, plus product-specific access_token values after Link/item creation. No first-party evidence was gathered for OAuth2, API keys, or HTTP Basic as Plaid API auth methods in the reviewed pages.
raw_result.gating_reason: Plaid publicly offers a free Sandbox environment, and its pricing page shows a self-serve Pay as You Go plan alongside sales-assisted Growth and Custom plans.

api_surface.breadth: Broad financial API covering account linking, transactions, identity, assets, auth, investments, liabilities, payments, transfer, webhooks, and related endpoints in Plaid’s HTTP/JSON API reference.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Evidence gathered indicates a Plaid GitHub MCP server repository exists (github.com/plaid/mcp-server), but no official plaid.com MCP documentation page was verified from the gathered evidence.

raw_result.blocker: Public docs and self-serve sandbox are sufficient to prototype, but production use and some higher tiers appear to involve sales-gated plans, and MCP availability was not verified on a first-party Plaid page.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Reviewed evidence indicates Plaid has rate-limit error documentation, but the browser run hit 404s/redirect issues when trying to open a stable canonical rate-limit page, so specific limits were not verified from the gathered pages.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs expose multiple environments including Sandbox and Production; pricing shows free sandbox plus self-serve Pay as You Go and higher commitment Growth/Custom options; broad developer docs and a public MCP GitHub repo are positive ecosystem signals.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Structured uncertainty remains for mcp_exists because the only gathered MCP evidence is a GitHub repository rather than a verified first-party Plaid page, and rate-limit documentation URLs encountered 404/redirect issues during browsing.
